Cloak of Jaggedpine  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: BACK
AC: 6
DEX: +3
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Cloak of Jaggedpine
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: BACK
AC: 6
DEX: +3
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Murdoc Dasilva
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:38:29
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 20
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Blackburrow Brewers

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Surefall Glade


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
This quest is still active
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2024 at 10:12 AM Rating: Decent
Taldas
Scholar
26 posts
I just completed this quest and I finally got my Cloak of Jaggedpine at level 49. I wear it to show my allegiance. So yes, it is still active. There is also a post from 2016 on the quest page that shows it being completed.

It took me 165 spawns to get 3 casks. I saw 20 gnoll brewers, 145 gnoll guardsman, and 0 master brewers. I also killed about 10 spawns before I started keeping track, which were all guardsmen. I saw brewers on spawn number 4, 7, 8, 29, 38, 49, 51, 55 (cask), 69, 92, 93 (cask), 109, 134, 138, 142, 145, 146, 147, 161, and 165 (cask). Spawn rates are between 13.5 minutes and 17.5 minutes usually. And yes, the brewer/guard spawns around -32,-32 and then wanders over to the barrels after maybe 10 minutes. They do eventually wander back to the water as well.

must suck to be on the new servers. The casks reguraly sell on Prexus for like 50-100p. i got 3 on my cleric as a souviner of my time in Blackburrow. brewers new pop local
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2002 at 10:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759911393
61 posts
unless verant changed it back recently, the brewer now pops up top as part of that high con roaming mob spawn that wanders back and forth from ef zone to the little cave behind the tree with the trap floor. so if you are looking to get brewer to spawn, kill off every grizzly, giant plague rat, gaurdsmen, or anything else you see wandering up top, eventually the brewer will pop in that spawn cycle. (oh, btw, its also the same spawn that yeilds tranixx on occasion) best of luck to you all. 8)

QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2003 at 7:15 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
The thing is, you can't always FIND a rogue to sell these to so you may be waiting a long time and if you need the stats this piece has, you're better to get it now rather than wait until you can sell the casks to someone else. Besides, if you are on a PvP server, you need no drop stuff, which this is. Would really suck to sell the casks and buya cloak only to have it taken the next time you get ganked.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
Success
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2002 at 3:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255825193
1 post
What this Druid did on her vacation

Day one: got up, ate a hearty breakfast and headed on into Blackburrow. Sat at the barrels from mid-morn till noon scoping out the lay of the burrow. Finally figured out the gnoll brewer and his ph (the guardsman) spawn left of the bottom of the bridge. Sat on the spawn from noon - late evening killing all the gobs in the area, collecting teeth, sellables and patches of gnoll fur to tailor. Gnoll brewer showed his mug approximately every 4th spawn but did not deem it necessary to leave me a cask.

Day two: ditto above expect Master Brewer showed his face and left me a cask. Sat on spawn watching the neighboring Shd/Wiz team ae everything in sight working on their faction. Looted their kills, made two bundled gnoll fur cloaks. (Switched out for my ac 2 rawhide cloak and gained two yellow bubs experience from turning in teeth and resulting moonstones. So a good day)

Day three: ditto above (except only one cloak tailored and half a bub yellow experience gained)
Two casks so far yeah.
Worked up Surefall faction porting the note and wooden statue back and forth.
Started Qeynos Badge Quest waiting for the 18 min spawns. (getting bored here)

Day four: Cask three dropped. Stated key words to Larsk Yeah he gave me the quest. Handed over my three hard earned casks with bated breath.

Success!!!!!!

▲ Page top

RE: Longer quest yet??

# Sep 09 2003 at 9:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783516037
124 posts
It would be nice if Sony updated this and other quests. They keep coming out with GREAT gear for Vah Shir and Iksar but have yet to do anything with the old races. At least change this from a no-drop item. Maybe with the new Augmentations it can actually become useful. On Vazaelle the Master Brewer is on about a six hour spawn timer, so getting three casks takes a LOT of patience. I have had this cloak for almost 18 months now, and it is eating up a slot in the bank. Come on Sony, UPGRADE IT!
▲ Page top
RE: Longer quest yet??
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2004 at 12:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787572093
Scholar
1,494 posts
Quote:
On Vazaelle the Master Brewer is on about a six hour spawn timer...


Unless there's something special about the Vazaelle server, this is incorrect. The cycle for the spawn is 18 minutes - I've timed it, while doing this quest myself. Note, however, that the Brewer won't show up every 18 minutes... it's more often a Guardsman. It seems to be a random spawn, roughly 20-25% chance of getting a Brewer - I've had him spawn back-to-back.

I spent 45 minutes camping the lake and not once did I get a brewer to pop. Do you realize how much that hurt. I'm wearing a 2ac cloak I would like to get the jaggedpine. And in my opinion it's a great cloak till lvl 20

Indalin 15th season Ranger
▲ Page top
RE: 45 minutes
QuoteReply
# Apr 17 2002 at 11:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761575884
Scholar
81 posts
Keep in mind that the brewers can pop in TWO places. FIrst place is the ridge by the lake to the right after the bridge. Second place is at the casks under the elite ledge. In both places, either a brewer or guardsman will pop. If you see a guardsman, kill it, and run to the other spawn point. Rinse, repeat. Good luck!
▲ Page top

them's tha breaks i guess. back to MM to try and level some more. on the upside i'm at apprehensive faction in qeynos(as a DE rogue that's no easy feat)

▲ Page top
Similar stat cloak
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2002 at 12:09 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591767079068
Sage
266 posts
If you are in BB anyway, killing gnolls, save the patches of gnoll fur. they can be combined in a sewing kit to make a gnoll fur bundle. it is ac5 and 5sv cold, if I recall properly. Unfortunately, it takes 64 patches of fur to make You combine them 4 at a time ,to make a gnoll fur patch. then you combine 4 gnoll fur patches to make a gnoll fur quarter (or something like that). combine 4 quarters, and you get a gnoll fur bundle, which is back slot wearable. While 64 patches of gnoll fur sounds like a lot, you can obtain them much faster than getting 3 casks. Also, if you are lazy or impatient, and have some cash, check the merchants in surefall glades. I purchased over a stack of patches from one merchant, at a price of 1sp and 3cp each. Making the cloak would cost less than 1pp, assuming that you don't have too many failures. At 88 tailoring, I only failed on one combine, and lost 4 patches. If you don't tailor, I'm sure you counld find someone to do the combines cheap, or free.

Anyway, make that cloak, and sell any casks you get to a rogue, and you'll be similarly equipped, and far richer.
▲ Page top
RE: Similar stat cloak
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2002 at 9:09 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250770727
Scholar
98 posts
This is the quest (as listed elsewhere on the boards):


"In the Halas bank on the second floor you will find Cappi Mctarnigal. Hail him and he will ask if you are a member of the white rose. When you tell him you are he asks you for mammoth calf hides but at the same time his fellow trainer, Vsanna MacGibbons, mentions gnoll fur. When you ask her what gnoll fur she tells you to take four patches of gnoll fur and sew them together then take four of this product and sew them together and finally take four of that and sew them together. What you end up with is a patched gnoll fur bundle. You can keep it or return it to her for the fang of the wolf."


The weapon is kinda lame (5/26) but the cloak is nice for a low-level, plus, you can build your tailoring skill up to at least "very bad" (21-30). Practically nobody ever wants the Patches of Gnoll Fur, because they bring in so little cash (under 8cp each, even with good CHA and faction), so most people trash them. Collect them! It's a free way to build your tailoring at a low level without having to pay for patterns!

You can buy a large (8 slot) sewing kit just outside Halas. I'm not sure if they're available in Qeynos.

- Place 4 unstacked patches in the kit, combine, results in stackable pieces
- Take 4 of these pieces, unstacked, and combine = gnoll fur quarter (forget exact name, these do not stack)
- Take 4 of these quarters and combine to make the cloak

The patches combine goes trivial around 15-20. The second step trivial is ?, as I am still having failures on this and not getting "you cannot raise!" messages. My guess would be maybe 30? Then the final combine would be slightly higher, of course. If you don't feel like hunting Blackburrow, it is very easy to pick up these patches off the vendors in Halas, Surefall, Qey Hills, and Qeynos, quite cheap.

▲ Page top
NEWS!!!
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2002 at 4:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253772649
1 post
Hello folks!

Another Ranger of Tunare proud to wear it.

I just completed this quest last nite.
And I notice that Larson didnt tell me that famous "you never know what bla, bla in the future..." So i guess Verant decided to finish with the doubt about it!!

My 2cp is that is a nice Ranger/Druid quest if
you like a bit of RPG for your char and you are aroun 15 lvl, but of course there better ones and more easy to get.

I spend a week (4 RL each day) and I just saw 4
brewers in 32,32. Any master. I got 1 cask from
one of them and the other two were given to me
from other players (thanks Realek and Cemamil),
I thank Tunare to still find nice people that dont ask me for 500pp for a cask for a quest!!!
Because i hardly have just 2 or 3 pp!!!

Just be patient, help people (they may help you
as happends to me), do some xp around and practice skills and spells, is a long time quest.

Be safe and let Tunare guide your path.
▲ Page top
bah the silly cloak
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2002 at 2:30 PM Rating: Decent
Adix
View User Forum
Sage
101 posts
All of that time spent on the quest and then I finally get the cloak. Two lvls later I started wishing I had not wasted the time - especially when I could have recieved up to 300plat per cask. Now at lvl 31 I assure you the cloak was not worth the time. Sell the casks unless you are really into wearing quested items - thats just my 2cp.

If you can not get the NPC to give you the quest have some one wolf form you - he is then happy to talk with you.
▲ Page top
Location, placeholders, spawn rate
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2002 at 3:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775830768
15 posts
Yes I agree, the spawn time for the normal brewers is between 18 and 19 minutes and the location is 39,39,-150. His placeholders are a gnoll guardsman most of the time, but occasionally, a patrolling gnoll will appear instead. The spawn rate is about 1 out of every 6 or so, with 1 out of every 4 brewers having a cask. I havent seen the Master Brewer but once, so im not sure where he spawns. It took me sevaral hours to get my three casks, but quite worth the loot considering I have 15 minutes or so to kill the commanders and such before the next pop.
▲ Page top
Pop Time and Question
QuoteReply
# Feb 15 2002 at 2:31 PM Rating: Good
Adix
View User Forum
Sage
101 posts
After spending 10 hours or so camping the brewer I have it down. He does pop next to the lake - and the spawn time is indeed 18 mins. There are placeholders for him and he seems to pop about 1/4 of the time - but i have counted as many as 8 place holders before he was up again. It seems to be random when he drops the cask, certainly it is not 1 in 4 as suggested! Still it is a fun quest. I set a timer for 17 mins after each kill and then ran around healing newbs for 16 minutes. They were all gratefull (all but one idiot who tried to KS and then wanted buffs after) and I would suggest doing so to any druid! Be SURE to have a high dam. spell on call as many ppl with try to KS for the barrels, I had three tries in one two hour session this week.

After all of the trouble to get the barrels I go to my man Just and he refuses to talk to me about the quest. So I give him the gnashers head and he says thanks and then mumbles something else about "when I am more trustworthy" - Anyone got an idea how I can get him to talk? If so please let me know.

Adix
23 Druid
▲ Page top
RE: Pop Time and Question
QuoteReply
# Mar 21 2002 at 4:38 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1712535405946
Sage
90 posts
Try going in treeform. Go and root right on his toes!
This worked for me when Chanda Miller wouldnt make me a bear skin cloak. So instead of "helping out her family" I just rooted and re-hailed her and it was no problem!

Goodluck. Everybody Loves trees.
( I am assuming this guy is outdoors... )
Havent tried to do quest so... I apologize if this is useless info.

__DEL__1591773230091

You have to have a better faction I suppose. Keep killing those gnolls and DON'T kill the bears and stuff in QH. Look at the bestiary for more faction info. I think you have to be amiable or higher.
▲ Page top
Got mine!
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2002 at 2:43 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591773230091
93 posts
To All you rogues out there, who say "Ur kwest iz stoopid, gmme cassk!" I say...Ha! I got my Cloak, and as a proud non-twink, I love it. I am one of few to have one. I know I could have gotten mucho loot for it, but I wanted the cloak, cause damn it, after 12 days of on and off camping, it feels good to know you did it.

BTW...the last Master Brewer popped up RIGHT in front of me when I was a little past underneath the bridge towards the pond. Easy Kill to LVL 17 RNG (or a lvl 30 ROG =P) If you go around asking the newbies fighting there many will just give you the cask if you play it cool.

Thanks to Vace, and to other forgotten named newbie that gave me the cask for a "Full set" of LVL 15 RNG Buffs and a heal!

Quimicolatino Busheater
Proud Wearer of The Cloak of Jaggedpine
▲ Page top
Hatemongers
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 4:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757886556
6 posts
I am so sick of flamers and hateful comments. You should go play on pvp and stay off the forums. Go victimize each other not the helpful people who post clues to solving these.

Some people do quests because it is fun, and you get faction and experience, and the problem was fun to solve. Some people even roleplay because it is an aesthetic meal.

If that is beyond your comprehension, I feel sad for you. Go kick a small animal like you were going to do anyway.

Wathe, 25 SK, Torvonillious.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 25 2001 at 11:46 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yeah! Eq is a Role-playing game, not a Uberloot/"I get my self worth from how much better my sword is then everyone elses" game. Play Uo or counterstrike, EQ will remain a RPG.
Cloak or Rapier...
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 5:08 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591763501175
Scholar
71 posts
My "Main" Characture is a druid (nluwen), And until recently when I started my rogue I never understood what the deal was, and had actualy thought about getting the cloak. After I made my rogue I did alot of research trying to find a good weapon(and other things), knowing that the bb cask was for a rogues quest also I attempted to find out what it was& did.

The Quest for the rogue is the Burning Rapier, its dmg/dly is 7/20 it has +5 Agi +5 Dex, and has Effect: Ignite(random proc at lvl 30)

The Quest for the Druid/Ranger has 6ac +3Dex

Both items are LORE and NO DROP..
So neither item would be went after more than once and the only real benefit would be the item itself, unless you think you can sell that no drop item you have ;).. So look at what the items give you in the end.

The importance of the rogue weapon is probably not clear to everyone, The Burning rapier is needed for the "Stanos' Pouch" sidequest in the rogue Epic Weapon quest(Ragebringer)

There are chances for much better cloaks in the game for druids and rangers than one with +3dex.

Since the 3Dex cloak is NO DROP, when you get a cloak better than this, you most likely store it away in the bank since your not able to sell it, and its not part of the epic, the only value it would have would be sentimental.

The Burning rapier will stay with the rogue who gets it im sure until they get the epic, and will probably be wielded for several levels after 30 when the Proc kicks in.

I agree with one thing, where if you are on pvp this is good because it is no drop. But for anyone else, there is better cloaks out there and you could probably make enough pp by sellin a couple casks to rogues to buy a good cloak. Everyone wins...
---
I have completed the Burning rapier quest on my rogue, I had a cask already before I knew about the quest , but if I had found out about the quest and not had the cask I would have payed for it if I needed to...

And as mentioned before by "Anonymous" 50pp is an acceptable price to me, Sell a couple of them and you can get yourself a good cloak as I Said a second ago.

But in the end its your choice what you do... I just hope my post was informative and gave a little insight into why rogues seek out this cask.
▲ Page top
RE: Cloak or Rapier...
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2002 at 4:14 PM Rating: Excellent
Kyrros
Sage
164 posts
Being a Ranger, I have done this quest, not for the cloak itself, but for its roleplay value.

I feel that any Ranger/Druid or Rogue is equally entitled to any of the brewer spawns.

Though, I will say, if you are a Ranger or Druid who doesn't really care about the roleplay/sentimental value of the cloak, I promise that it is easier to just sell the cask (but only to Rogues... anyone else is just farming 'Commons' fodder to sell) for 50pp.. take the upper tunnel to Everfrost Peaks and /auction for a 'Cloak of the Icebear' which is a common sell for an even 50pp off of the Icebear brothers. The cloack is 8AC (1AC better than banded) and +20 sv Cold.

The fact is, I am STILL wearing my Cloak of the Icebear, and I am level 30 now :)
____________________________
Kyrros <Knights Of Insomnia> - Bristlebane
Guild Leader and Guild Historian
Human Veteran Ranger and Warder of Tunare for 70 80 85 90 seasons
Knights of Insomnia Homepage - Hosted by Guildportal.com
http://www.guildportal.com/Guild.aspx?GuildID=2542&TabID=16569
▲ Page top
Cask Camping
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2001 at 7:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764068958
3 posts
I'm tired of hearing rogues whine about the camp, I don't care if the cask "seems" more valuable to a rogue than a druid or ranger. It is valuable to whoever wants to take the time to complete a quest. Face it EQ is about camping, you think you casks are bad...try an FBSS. So all you rogues who like to pull from camps and KS with back stab, just have a little patience, or ask the person camping if you can have the camp for a bit. if not respect him, same for rangers and druids. Just respect peoples camps and EQ will be better all around

perhaps this is used for another quest?
QuoteReply

__DEL__1592248459345

From what i gather from reading the above and about the future enchantments it sounds as if this is used in a quest to get a better item. This would also explain why this item is no drop. Just giving my 2cp.

# Jun 09 2001 at 7:03 PM Rating: Decent

View User Forum

If you arent on Rallos Zek, this cloak is crap. Let the rogues have the casks. But if you're on Rallos Zek, the cloak becomes VERY useful because it cannot be looted. Another cloak that is just as good is easy to get, but no one can take this one away from you.
